title: Test enforceable Apache-2.0 licensing and author attribution

claims.statement: Before CHG-0023, the repository, public landing, package manifests, and published protocol tarball declared MIT, while public renderer and UI package roots did not carry their own license and attribution artifacts.
claims.evidence: LICENSE, README.md, landing/index.html, workspace package.json files, and public package roots before CHG-0023

claims.statement: The Apache Software Foundation publishes Apache License 2.0 as reusable by non-ASF projects and documents a top-level LICENSE plus NOTICE attribution as the distribution pattern.

The human owner directs OverlayKit to use Apache License 2.0 and attribute the work to Rodrigo Vicente with the public identity @rodrigoteamx, and represents sufficient authority to relicense the current clean-history work.

H-014: One byte-exact official Apache-2.0 license, one canonical NOTICE attribution, consistent SPDX and author metadata, packaged legal artifacts, visible repository and landing attribution, and a deterministic check in the enforced quality command can prevent supported source and npm distributions from silently drifting back to MIT or omitting Rodrigo Vicente attribution.
claims.evidence: SDD-014 hypothesis authorized for implementation by Human Doctrine directive on 2026-07-21

claims.statement: Human review accepted H-014 only for deterministic consistency and enforcement across supported repository surfaces and npm package payloads after exact LICENSE and NOTICE checks, all manifest and lockfile checks, packed-package evidence, hostile verifier tests, landing inspection, full repository checks, builds, and audit passed.
claims.evidence: Human Doctrine acceptance on 2026-07-21 after SDD-014 refutation evidence

claims.statement: The accepted Slice does not establish ownership of third-party contributions, trademark registration, contributor license agreements, dependency-license compatibility, software-bill-of-materials completeness, package-registry publication, legal enforceability in a jurisdiction, or whether historical artifacts outside this clean-history repository may be relicensed.

successCriteria.statement: The repository and every public npm package root contain a byte-exact copy of the official Apache License 2.0 and the canonical NOTICE naming Rodrigo Vicente and linking @rodrigoteamx.
successCriteria.verification: SHA-256 and exact NOTICE checks in npm run check:licensing.

successCriteria.statement: Every workspace manifest and lockfile entry declares SPDX Apache-2.0, every manifest attributes Rodrigo Vicente with the X URL, and public package allowlists include LICENSE and NOTICE.
successCriteria.verification: Manifest, lockfile, allowlist, and packed-package assertions.

successCriteria.statement: README, package documentation, contribution terms, and landing content communicate Apache-2.0 and visible Rodrigo Vicente attribution without a current MIT claim.
successCriteria.verification: Deterministic public-surface scan and landing build.

successCriteria.statement: Licensing verification executes inside npm run check and fails before quality evidence can pass when supported legal artifacts or metadata drift.
successCriteria.verification: Root script composition plus hostile verifier subprocess tests or direct failure probes.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0023 records observed facts, the owner directive, H-014, measurable criteria, the accepted bounded inference, and unresolved legal guarantees without asserting more than the evidence supports.

The Slice changes only licensing, attribution, package-distribution metadata and tests, visible documentation, the licensing quality check, and the governance records required to capture the accepted evidence.
Review attempts to refute H-014 across exact license bytes, NOTICE consistency, all workspace manifests, lockfile state, public package payloads, public text surfaces, and quality-gate composition.
definitionOfDone.evidence: Licensing verifier, package-distribution tests, package dry runs, and repository checks

definitionOfDone.statement: Governance, licensing, package distribution, full repository checks, build, landing verification, and audit pass for the accepted Slice manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check, npm run check:licensing, protocol package tests, npm run check, npm run build, and npm audit

definitionOfDone.statement: ADR-0018 and the bounded inference are created only after the human authority reviews the refutation evidence and explicitly accepts H-014.
definitionOfDone.evidence: Human Doctrine acceptance on 2026-07-21 and ADR-0018
